prompt:
We're adding 2048 to the office game night kiosk and I need the engine to be replay-exact: same seed plus same move script must give the same board on every machine, byte for byte, because the kiosk stores games as (seed, moves) pairs. Build game2048.ts (stdlib only, no npm); test_2048.ts is the contract and you don't edit it.

Game state is plain data: { board, score, state, won } where board is a 4x4 number[][] with 0 for empty, state is the current RNG state, and won is a sticky flag. The RNG is the classic 31-bit LCG: state' = (state * 1103515245 + 12345) mod 2^31, exported as lcg(state). Watch the arithmetic — that product overflows double precision, so multiply exactly (BigInt or split the multiply); lcg(2147483647) has one right answer.

Spawning: enumerate the free cells row-major (top row left-to-right first). Draw once — the new tile's position is the draw modulo the free-cell count. Draw again — the tile is a 4 when that draw is divisible by 10, else a 2. newGame(seed) starts from state = seed mod 2^31 (non-negative), an empty board, then spawns two tiles that way, so exactly four draws are consumed.

Merging is where every knock-off gets it wrong, so it's pinned: export slide(line) which takes one 4-cell line, compacts the non-zeros toward index 0, then merges equal adjacent pairs starting from the near edge — a tile created by a merge can NEVER merge again in the same move — and returns { line, gained } where gained is the sum of the tiles created. [2,2,2,2] is [4,4,0,0] gaining 8; [2,2,4,0] is [4,4,0,0] gaining 4, not an 8. move(game, dir) with dir 'left' | 'right' | 'up' | 'down' applies that law with the traversal anchored at the edge you push toward (right reverses rows, up runs columns top-down, down bottom-up), adds gained to score, and — only if the board actually changed — spawns exactly one tile with the rule above. A move that changes nothing returns the EXACT SAME game object: no spawn, no RNG advance. move never mutates its argument. Unknown directions throw.

won flips to true as soon as any tile reaches 2048 and stays true; the game keeps playing after that. gameOver(game) is true only when the board is full and no two orthogonal neighbors are equal — and every move on such a board is a same-object no-op.

play(seed, moves) replays a script of 'L' 'R' 'U' 'D' letters from newGame(seed), treating impossible moves as no-ops and throwing on any other letter. render(game) returns the four rows joined with \n, each row its cells right-aligned to width 4 ('.' for empty) joined with '|', no trailing newline.

node --test test_2048.ts must pass as shipped.
